reassignments.to_canonical_name: Incidence, prevalence, and burden estimation from surveillance or survey data
reassignments.rationale: Administrative linkage is used to estimate mortality burden and rates, not to evaluate a surveillance platform.
This is a systematic review and meta-analysis of STI prevalence evidence.
It mines vaccine adverse-event clusters for safety surveillance.
The core contribution is that standard genital-only testing misses rectal infections under prevailing case-finding definitions.
This directly measures underreporting of notified TB cases to the national system.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Incidence, prevalence, and burden estimation from surveillance or survey data
reassignments.rationale: The study estimates population attack rates and hospitalization burden for RSV versus influenza.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance data validation and completeness assessment
reassignments.rationale: It audits completeness of HIV testing and HIV-status fields in the TB database.
Age-specific IFR estimation is built from prevalence surveys and explicitly interpreted through death underreporting adjustment.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system evaluation and reporting-quality studies
reassignments.rationale: It evaluates capture and limitations of a laboratory surveillance system for vaccine-impact assessment.
Cross-sectional telephone surveys are used as a complementary influenza monitoring stream.
The paper reconstructs incidence and case detection from time-stamped viral genomes for surveillance use.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system evaluation and reporting-quality studies
reassignments.rationale: It compares active household surveillance with facility surveillance to assess sensitivity and representativeness.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Counterfactual policy and vaccination impact models
reassignments.rationale: Hidden-burden estimation is used to study how underreporting changes vaccination-strategy effects.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Incidence, prevalence, and burden estimation from surveillance or survey data
reassignments.rationale: It combines surveillance and administrative data to estimate true IPD incidence burden.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system design, reporting practice, and measurement frameworks
reassignments.rationale: The contribution is a conceptual/organizational framework for how local actors shape surveillance information flow.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Simulation and decision-analytic models for burden or policy planning
reassignments.rationale: This is a decision-analytic transmission and cost-effectiveness model for vaccination policy.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system improvement and reporting-enhancement strategies
reassignments.rationale: The paper centers on strengthening surveillance capacity and outbreak response through workforce development.
It estimates pediatric TB incidence together with age- and country-specific case-detection ratios.
A hidden-population N-mixture framework infers underreported COVID counts from partial epidemic observations.
Seroprevalence is used to estimate actual infection burden and quantify reporting gaps.
It improves epidemic parameter estimation by combining compartmental modeling with Gaussian-process smoothing under noisy underreported data.
Mathematical transmission models are used to estimate hidden infections and deaths from scarce reports.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Incidence, prevalence, and burden estimation from surveillance or survey data
reassignments.rationale: This is a seroprevalence-based estimate of infection burden in the population.
Prospective symptom diaries are used as active surveillance to capture mild respiratory infections.
This is a systematic review and meta-analysis of vaccine effectiveness against Delta.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system evaluation and reporting-quality studies
reassignments.rationale: It evaluates AFP surveillance indicators, laboratory performance, and underreporting across countries.
The paper studies how underreporting distorts spatiotemporal COVID distributions across places and periods.
It integrates adjusted seroprevalence and vaccination data to estimate population immunity under infection undercount.
A mechanistic transmission model is refined to represent passive detection changes, underreporting, and intervention projections.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Evidence syntheses on surveillance, reporting, and hidden burden
reassignments.rationale: This review synthesizes pertussis surveillance, burden, and reporting gaps across Asian countries.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system improvement and reporting-enhancement strategies
reassignments.rationale: The qualitative study identifies system barriers and facilitators for local epidemic response including surveillance flaws.
It uses global spontaneous adverse-drug-reaction reports for safety signal assessment.
The study directly quantifies underreporting of HIV-related deaths in mortality records.
Excess mortality attributable to COVID is estimated while accounting for underdiagnosis and underreporting.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Spatial risk mapping with detection/reporting adjustment
reassignments.rationale: The epidemic risk index is externally validated using accessibility-based correction for underreporting.
This is a descriptive analysis of cholera spatial distribution across temporal fragments.
Transmission modeling plus serosurveys are used to re-estimate attack rate and death reporting completeness.
The paper synthesizes child/adolescent TB policy-practice gaps centered on under-detection, reporting, and system strengthening.
SEIR-fansy explicitly models false negatives and selection into testing/reporting.
It measures prevalence and nonreporting of occupational bloodborne exposures.
This is a formal evaluation of an AEFI surveillance system and its underreporting drivers.
It analyzes AEFI reporting rates, completeness, and reaction profiles from surveillance records.
The study links pandemic containment effectiveness to economic growth while allowing for COVID underreporting.
Outpatient pharmacy dispensing is used as a complementary signal to evaluate TB reporting decline.
A Bayesian spatial hierarchical model corrects state-level COVID misreporting.
District-level prevalence-to-notification ratios are mapped to identify spatial surveillance gaps.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Risk-factor and intervention studies with reporting-bias implications
reassignments.rationale: This is a causal safety study where exposure underreporting is treated as a potential bias source, not the estimand.
This is a systematic review of human dirofilariasis epidemiology and burden.
It estimates daily and cumulative SARS-CoV-2 infections by integrating multiple biased data sources with correction for underreporting.
This review synthesizes invasive candidiasis burden and reporting patterns across Africa.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Incidence, prevalence, and burden estimation from surveillance or survey data
reassignments.rationale: The study estimates seroprevalence of zoonotic agents in dromedaries to quantify burden and risk.
Postmortem autopsy screening uncovers otherwise missed SARS-CoV-2 infections and deaths.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance data validation and completeness assessment
reassignments.rationale: It validates registry capture and reporting accuracy against confirmed infection cases.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Incidence, prevalence, and burden estimation from surveillance or survey data
reassignments.rationale: This is a global burden study estimating mortality and DALY rates, with underreporting noted as a burden limitation.
Federal-state seroprevalence data are used to estimate infection rate, IFR, and underreporting factor.
Reported resistance rates are shown to depend on outdated breakpoint definitions, creating reporting misclassification.
Excess mortality is used to reconstruct transmission dynamics and hidden infection/death burden.
It is a structured evidence review of sequelae burden and underreported outcomes for economic evaluation.
The study analyzes national and global pharmacovigilance databases for PrEP adverse events.
A natural experiment estimates the effect of mandatory school testing on measured incidence via reduced underreporting.
Twitter is mined as a complementary stream for vaccine adverse-event safety signal detection.
This ecological study estimates COVID underdiagnosis within SARI surveillance records.
The paper describes subdistrict spatiotemporal spread and Rt under near-complete screening rather than hidden-burden correction.
Wastewater measurements are evaluated for prevalence estimation, hotspot detection, and variant surveillance.
A fixed network of maternity units is used as a facility-based surveillance platform to estimate baseline adverse outcome rates.
The core method is transmission-lineage reconstruction and genomic epidemic characterization.
This is a systematic review and meta-analysis of injury prevalence, risk factors, and reporting practice.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Risk-factor and intervention studies with reporting-bias implications
reassignments.rationale: It is a case-control study of infection prevention risk factors for SARS-CoV-2 infection.
The paper quantifies disease reductions attributable to routine childhood immunization using adjusted incidence estimates.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Self-report and behavioral measurement bias assessment
reassignments.rationale: It studies omission of network ties as a self-report measurement bias affecting transmission-relevant network metrics.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system design, reporting practice, and measurement frameworks
reassignments.rationale: This is a framework paper on how to design and harmonize ARV safety surveillance in pregnancy and breastfeeding.
It profiles SARI surveillance records to identify likely COVID underreporting and its predictors.
Large-scale wastewater surveillance is used to infer prevalence ahead of clinical reporting.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system evaluation and reporting-quality studies
reassignments.rationale: Mortality audit narratives are analyzed to identify system factors shaping observed COVID outcomes and reporting context.
It models determinants of empirical reporting rates across pathogens and countries to characterize hidden burden.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance ascertainment, validation, and correction studies
reassignments.rationale: The study evaluates how subgroup-specific testing positivity should correct RSV under-ascertainment.
This is an individual-based stochastic network SIR model with undercount estimation and policy counterfactuals.
Prospective space-time scan statistics are used to detect and describe active COVID clusters.
A dynamic SIRD model jointly forecasts epidemic states and time-varying underreporting.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Incidence, prevalence, and burden estimation from surveillance or survey data
reassignments.rationale: Three linked military health data sources are combined to improve STI burden estimation.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance data validation and completeness assessment
reassignments.rationale: The linkage algorithm improves completeness and subgroup identification within HIV surveillance-related data.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system improvement and reporting-enhancement strategies
reassignments.rationale: The paper describes concrete surveillance strengthening activities and capacity-building lessons.
It studies within-host and transmitted sequence diversity as a genomic transmission phenomenon.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Counterfactual policy and vaccination impact models
reassignments.rationale: Age-structured mechanistic models are used to compare vaccination-prioritization strategies and their epidemic impact.
Differential-equation epidemic models are used to estimate African underreporting and mitigation effects.
SEIR dynamics are coupled with Kalman/moving-horizon state estimation to infer reporting changes and variant effects.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system design and reporting-improvement studies
reassignments.rationale: It designs and evaluates a fever-clinic management process to improve detection and avoid missed reporting.
Serosurveillance and postmortem surveillance are combined to quantify hidden COVID incidence in Africa.
This review synthesizes influenza surveillance and vaccine-effectiveness evidence over the decade.
Combined household and health-service case finding is used to estimate a neglected burden more completely than passive sources.
This is a scoping review synthesizing mortality-inequality evidence and reporting limitations.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Evidence syntheses on surveillance, reporting, and hidden burden
reassignments.rationale: The integrative review is explicitly about determinants and implications of monkeypox underestimation.
A spatiotemporal Bayesian model estimates local underreported COVID counts and detection rates.
Retrospective case finding is used to detect missed TBE diagnoses in emerging risk areas.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Simulation and decision-analytic models for burden or policy planning
reassignments.rationale: This is a cost-utility decision model comparing maternal pertussis immunization strategies.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Self-report and behavioral measurement bias assessment
reassignments.rationale: The study focuses on mechanisms of under-reporting sexual behavior in donor screening.
Burden-of-illness methods with underreporting multipliers estimate illnesses averted and economic savings.
This is a living systematic review of corticosteroid effectiveness and safety evidence.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance data validation and completeness assessment
reassignments.rationale: It quantifies normal, under-, and over-reporting in routine immunization data systems.
Country COVID death burden is ranked under explicit uncertainty about case fatality and underreporting.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Counterfactual policy and vaccination impact models
reassignments.rationale: The model studies vaccine rollout, waning, behavior, variants, and reopening under case undercount.
It estimates real-time transmission heterogeneity jointly with reproduction number from incidence series.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Simulation and decision-analytic models for burden or policy planning
reassignments.rationale: Monte Carlo QMRA is used to estimate hidden illness burden from contaminated private wells for management decisions.
This is a systematic review of healthcare-associated hepatitis outbreaks and reporting gaps.
A nationally representative survey provides an alternative source to model hidden COVID burden beyond routine reports.
Time-series epidemic modeling is used to quantify the impact of precautionary behaviors on influenza transmission.
The study traces incomplete stool collection/testing to quantify CDI underdiagnosis and underreporting.
This is a global public-health and economic impact evaluation of vaccination using underreporting-adjusted counts.
Serology is used to estimate pertussis incidence and demonstrate large reporting gaps.
A qualitative systems study identifies structural and behavioral barriers to AEFI reporting.
This implementation audit targets improvement of COVID vaccine AEFI reporting practice.
Citizen-science bird observations are integrated as a complementary surveillance input for avian influenza.
A mechanistic Zika transmission model explicitly estimates the degree of under-reporting and intervention effects.
This scoping review synthesizes outbreak transmission routes and risk factors for giardiasis.
An age-structured transmission model incorporates age-specific underdetection to estimate who drives spread.
The central issue is whether telemedicine under-detects surgical site infection relative to in-person assessment.
This systematic review synthesizes global evidence on asymptomatic Leishmania infection incidence.
The paper is a descriptive spatial hotspot and temporal-trend analysis of childhood TB.
It reviews the epidemiologic role of wild suids and reporting gaps in ASF spread.
Spatial autocorrelation and scan statistics are used to describe pertussis clustering.
The paper estimates the immunologically naive population by integrating vaccination with under-ascertained infection history.
This is wastewater-surveillance methodology for back-estimation, focusing on in-sewer decay and partitioning.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Self-report and behavioral measurement bias assessment
reassignments.rationale: The study explicitly adjusts survey responses for reporting bias in vaccination intent.
This is a systematic review and meta-analysis of histoplasmosis epidemiology and burden in Nigeria.
It is a structured evidence review emphasizing reporting gaps for infection-related secondary immunodeficiency outcomes.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system evaluation and reporting-quality studies
reassignments.rationale: The study evaluates healthcare-worker knowledge, practice, and feedback constraints within a notifiable disease surveillance system.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Evidence syntheses on surveillance, reporting, and hidden burden
reassignments.rationale: This scoping review centers on scarcity and underreporting of HIV surveillance data in MENA.
Seroprevalence is compared with notifications to quantify pertussis underreporting.
The study suggests standard blood-culture-based detection misses bloodstream infection events reflected by catheter contamination.
An automated register-based system is built to detect hospital infection clusters beyond mandatory notifications.
Reported cases are integrated with seroprevalence in a time-varying epidemic model to infer testing efficiency and underreporting.
This is a meta-analysis synthesizing undercount-adjusted national IFR estimates.
A misreporting-aware non-stationary time-series framework reconstructs the hidden COVID trajectory.
Self-reported HIV testing, status, and treatment are validated against clinic records, quantifying over- and under-reporting.
This is a systematic review of outbreak investigation practices and reporting gaps after CRPA detection.
Antenatal serum samples are used as a structured sentinel platform for repeated seroprevalence surveillance.
This scoping review synthesizes rabies burden, surveillance, and elimination progress evidence.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system evaluation and reporting-quality studies
reassignments.rationale: The paper assesses how laboratory diagnostic capacity constrains gonorrhea case reporting.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Incidence, prevalence, and burden estimation from surveillance or survey data
reassignments.rationale: It estimates long-COVID prevalence and shows large undercount from code-based ascertainment.
Outbreak data are scaled by underreporting and underdiagnosis multipliers to estimate true foodborne burden.
This systematic review synthesizes how public health emergencies affect notification, surveillance, and disease-control systems.
Repeated blood-donor serosurveillance is used to monitor prevalence and infer underreporting over time.
A frontier-based epidemic model jointly estimates documented and undocumented cases from observed counts.
It estimates reporting-chain multipliers to convert reported symptomatic cases into total community infections.
The review is about systematic under-reporting of research-governance and data-handling practices as a study-quality problem.
Burial reports, cemetery imagery, and social-media surveys are triangulated as alternative epidemic indicators.
The study estimates AEFI spontaneous-reporting rates and predictors in COVID vaccine surveillance.
Bayesian inference over serosurveys and reported cases estimates time-varying infection underreporting.
Wastewater signals are integrated with a compartmental model to estimate prevalence and clinical diagnostic bias.
Biomarkers are compared with self-report to validate the extent of alcohol-use underreporting.
